title: Cyber Attack Tolerance
objective: Maintain operational resilience against cyber attacks while enabling business innovation
proposedRTS: The organization accepts a residual cyber risk score of up to 12 (Medium) for standard operations, with enhanced monitoring and quarterly review for scores 10-12
rationale: Balances security investment with operational agility. Aligns with industry benchmarks for financial services sector

title: Data Protection Tolerance
objective: Protect sensitive data while enabling data-driven business operations
proposedRTS: The organization maintains near-zero tolerance for personal data breaches. Maximum acceptable residual score of 8 for data-related risks
rationale: GDPR requirements and reputational sensitivity demand stringent data protection. Customer trust is paramount

title: Service Availability Tolerance
objective: Ensure business continuity while managing infrastructure investment
proposedRTS: The organization accepts occasional service degradation with RTO ≤4 hours and RPO ≤1 hour for critical systems. Residual score up to 10 acceptable
rationale: Cost-benefit analysis supports current DR investment level. Meets regulatory and contractual SLAs

title: Third-Party Risk Tolerance
objective: Enable vendor partnerships while managing supply chain risk
proposedRTS: The organization accepts residual third-party risk scores up to 12 for non-critical vendors and up to 8 for critical vendors
rationale: Business operations depend on vendor ecosystem. Risk-based tiering enables proportionate management

title: Regulatory Compliance Tolerance
objective: Maintain compliance while managing compliance costs efficiently
proposedRTS: Zero tolerance for material regulatory non-compliance. Maximum residual score of 6 for compliance-related risks
rationale: Regulatory penalties and reputational damage potential exceeds cost of compliance. License to operate

title: Insider Threat Tolerance
objective: Balance employee privacy with security monitoring
proposedRTS: The organization accepts residual insider threat risk scores up to 10 with enhanced monitoring for privileged users
rationale: Employee trust important for culture. Monitoring capabilities provide detection confidence

title: Cloud Security Tolerance
objective: Enable cloud adoption while managing cloud-specific risks
proposedRTS: The organization accepts cloud security residual scores up to 10 with mandatory CSPM monitoring and quarterly configuration reviews
rationale: Cloud benefits outweigh risks with proper controls. Industry moving to cloud-first

title: ICT Concentration Tolerance
proposedRTS: Maximum 40% dependency on any single ICT provider. Residual concentration risk score must be ≤8
rationale: DORA requirements mandate concentration management. Operational resilience critical

title: Financial Impact Tolerance
proposedRTS: Single incident loss tolerance: €2M. Annual aggregate loss tolerance: €5M. Risk scores translating to potential losses exceeding these require Board approval
rationale: Aligns with insurance coverage and capital reserves. Board-approved thresholds

title: Reputational Risk Tolerance
proposedRTS: Near-zero tolerance for incidents likely to generate negative media coverage. Maximum residual score of 6 for reputation-impacting risks
rationale: Trust is foundational to business. Reputational damage recovery slow and expensive
